answers: Precision agriculture uses GPS, sensors, drones, and data analytics to optimize crop production. It applies the right inputs at the right place and time for maximum efficiency.
Precision agriculture holo technology use kore exact jayga te exact porimane fertilizer, pani, pesticide dewa. Drone, GPS, sensor — shob use hoy.
Precision farming technologies include GPS-guided tractors, drone imaging, soil sensors, satellite monitoring, and AI-powered crop analysis. It reduces waste and increases yields significantly.
Precision agriculture helps farmers make data-driven decisions, reducing chemical use by up to 90%, saving water, and increasing crop yields while minimizing environmental impact.
